tasks.description:
## What
Document and operationalize Borg's two-phase delivery pattern as a repeatable methodology.

## Phase 1: Iterate with client (spike/discovery)
- Hand drawings → HTML prototypes, rapidly iterated
- Refine UI + OWL + value stream + domains as we go
- 2-3 cycles
- Identify integrations, security, and harvest considerations along the way
- Output: validated prototype that captures intent

## Phase 2: Build from 1 (commitment)
- The iteration output becomes the spec — estimable, buildable
- Code + tests + UX + tickets + logs + monitoring + security + documentation
- Full blast radius coverage (DEC-072)

## Why
This is Jeff's natural working pattern — validated by the Athena experience at Staples. Phase 1 is discovery through making. Phase 2 is commitment informed by understanding. Maps to spike/discovery/commitment taxonomy.

## Athena lesson
Process weight kills adoption. Phase 1 must stay lightweight — sketches, conversation, rapid cycles. Phase 2 inherits rigor from the system, not from human paperwork.

## AC
- [ ] Pattern documented in About library
- [ ] Card lifecycle integrates the two phases (spike → discovery → commitment maps to Phase 1 → Phase 2)
- [ ] Borg can execute Phase 1 autonomously given hand drawings as input

## Tags
[medium] [discovery] chorus methodology

tasks.description:
## Experience
I report a bug and the role pushes back — "it's not a bug" or "that's expected behavior." Then I'm stuck relaying between the role and the problem, burning attention on coordination instead of creative work. I expect Wren to handle that friction. When a role explains away my bug report, Wren should reject the explanation and re-nudge, not bring the debate to me. I only want to hear about a problem when it's fixed or when Wren is genuinely stuck.

When a role pushes back on a bug report or rejects feedback, Wren defers to Jeff instead of handling it. Jeff becomes the relay and escalation point for every friction. This is the highest-cost failure mode in the system.

## AC
- [ ] When a role says 'it's not a bug' or explains away a problem Jeff reported, Wren rejects and re-nudges — does not relay the explanation to Jeff
- [ ] Roles nudge each other for help before escalating to Jeff — Wren enforces this
- [ ] 'I fixed it' always gets 'show me' — no trust without evidence, Wren verifies before telling Jeff
- [ ] Jeff only hears about a problem when it's actually fixed or Wren is genuinely blocked
- [ ] When Jeff walks away, coordination continues — roles don't stop working because Jeff stopped watching

## Experience

When the team moves a directory, everything breaks because 644 absolute paths across 301 files all point to the old location. Services fail silently until restarted. I need one place to change the repo root path so restructures don't cascade into hours of recovery.

## AC

- [ ] CHORUS_ROOT env var defined — single source of truth for repo root path
- [ ] All Rust source (33+ files in chorus-hooks) uses CHORUS_ROOT instead of hardcoded /Users/jeffbridwell/CascadeProjects/

- [ ] All LaunchAgent plists (32+ files) use wrapper scripts that set CHORUS_ROOT
- [ ] ~/.claude/settings.json hooks path uses CHORUS_ROOT
- [ ] Symlinks in platform/scripts/ are relative, not absolute
- [ ] Smoke test: change CHORUS_ROOT value, verify services resolve paths correctly
- [ ] Kade test suite passes

- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/**/*.rs (33 files)
- platform/scripts/*.sh (43 files)
- platform/api/src/server.ts
- directing/clearing/src/server.ts
- platform/board-client/src/config.ts, client.ts
- ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.chorus.*.plist, com.gathering.*.plist (32 files)

tasks.description:
## Experience
When I start a new session with a role, I have no idea what they did last time. I have to ask, or dig through git logs. The session-start file should just tell me — here's what this role shipped, struggled with, and left open.

## AC
- [ ] session-start script includes a "Last Session" section per role
- [ ] Section shows: cards completed, cards in progress, key decisions made, open blockers
- [ ] Data sourced from git log + activity.md + board state, not cached summaries
- [ ] Each role's session-start file includes their own last session AND a 1-liner for the other two roles
- [ ] Works correctly after multi-day gaps (not just "yesterday")

tasks.title: Auto-declare role state from card actions — pull emits building, done emits idle, no manual role-state calls
tasks.description:
## Experience
I look at gemba or team-scan and see a role actively working but listed as idle or unknown. The state declaration is manual — roles forget to call role-state. Scolding doesn't fix a manual process. The system should enforce it.

## AC
- [ ] board pull/move to WIP auto-emits role-state building card=<id>
- [ ] board done/move to Done auto-emits role-state idle
- [ ] session start emits role-state idle
- [ ] gemba-start and team-scan reflect accurate state without manual role-state calls
- [ ] Remove manual role-state calls from CLAUDE.md instructions where they duplicate automated behavior

tasks.title: Skill step enforcement — gate terminal actions on spine events, not trust
tasks.description:
## Experience
I watch a role run /demo and they skip half the steps. The skill describes the protocol but nothing enforces it. 50% improvisation rate on /demo alone. If I can't trust that a skill was followed, the skill is documentation not process.

## AC
- [ ] Each skill step emits a spine event when executed (e.g., demo.smoke_check.completed)
- [ ] Terminal actions (accept, done, merge) check that prerequisite skill steps fired
- [ ] Missing steps block the terminal action with a clear message ("demo smoke check not recorded")
- [ ] /demo is the first skill wired — smoke check, prep summary, signal to Wren all gated
- [ ] Instrumentation is in the skill runner, not in each role's CLAUDE.md

tasks.title: Commit discipline — roles committing test artifacts, undeclared work, and board pollution
tasks.description:
## Experience
The commit history and board are full of test card artifacts (200+ BDD test cards in Won't Do), commits from roles working without cards pulled, and state that doesn't match what's actually happening. The commits tell a scary story about process discipline.

## AC
- [ ] Audit Won't Do column — bulk-close or delete test artifact cards (BDD test card, CLI test card, etc.)
- [ ] Pre-commit gate: reject commits when role has no WIP card declared (ties to #2323)
- [ ] Board hygiene sweep — remove duplicate and test-artifact cards from all columns
- [ ] Document what a clean commit looks like (role prefix, card reference, what changed)

tasks.title: Peer review skill — audit role awareness, process compliance, and system enforcement gaps
tasks.description:
## Experience
When I check in on a role, I'm not watching them work (gemba) — I'm asking: do you know what you did last time? Are you following the process? Is the system actually enforcing what we think? This is a different interaction pattern that doesn't have a skill yet.

## AC
- [ ] /review <role> skill defined with steps: last-session awareness check, state declaration check, WIP card check, recent commit audit
- [ ] Outputs a scorecard: what's working, what's drifting, what the system should enforce but doesn't
- [ ] Findings auto-card as fix cards when they reveal enforcement gaps
- [ ] Distinct from /gemba (real-time observation) — this is diagnostic

tasks.title: Session health alerts route to own role only — not Jeff, not other roles
tasks.description:
## Experience
I get session-health alerts about Silas and Kade's context pressure in my terminal. It's noise — I don't need to know their memory state, and forwarding it to them just adds to the noise. Each role should handle their own session health silently.

## AC
- [ ] session-health alerts route only to the role they're about
- [ ] Jeff never sees session-health alerts in his terminal
- [ ] Other roles never see session-health alerts about each other
- [ ] Role handles its own reboot decision without external nudging

tasks.title: Repo cleanup — rename gathering-team, flatten chorus/ to root, remove symlinks, resolve split-brain role dirs, delete cruft
tasks.description:
## Experience

When I look at the repo structure, I see symlinks pretending to be directories, two copies of every role directory that have diverged, 500MB of dead backups and abandoned files, and a repo named gathering-team that only contains chorus/. The name doesn't match the content, the structure doesn't match reality, and the cruft slows down every push. After this, the repo is clean, correctly named, and the directory structure matches what git actually tracks.

## AC

- [ ] Rename gathering-team repo to chorus on GitHub. Update local remote URL.
- [ ] Flatten chorus/ subdirectory to repo root using git filter-repo (preserves history).
- [ ] Remove 4 top-level symlinks (architect, engineer, product-manager, messages). Replace with additionalDirectories in settings.local.json.
- [ ] Resolve split-brain role directories — one canonical location per role (chorus/platform/roles/<name> vs chorus/<role-name>). Delete the duplicate.
- [ ] Delete dead weight: architect-old/, chorus.bak.*, gathering-team/ stub, top-level loose scripts, stale chorus-sdk/.
- [ ] Update all hardcoded paths in: state_paths.rs (REPO_ROOT), skills (acp, reboot), LaunchAgent plists, git-queue.sh.
- [ ] All 3 roles can boot, commit, push, and deliver briefs after the migration.
- [ ] No symlinks required for normal operation.

## Files

- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shared/state_paths.rs
- chorus/platform/scripts/git-queue.sh
- chorus/platform/skills/acp/SKILL.md
- chorus/platform/skills/reboot/skill.md
- chorus/architect/.claude/settings.local.json
- chorus/engineer/.claude/settings.local.json
- chorus/product-manager/.claude/settings.local.json
